body:
## Summary

The current `scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` enforces a hard 1500-line limit but has **no warn band**. Two PRs that each individually keep a file under 1500 can together push it over after both merge — the second PR's CI passes pre-merge, then the post-merge check on `main` is the first failure signal. Downstream Docker / Helm / Release jobs are then gated and skipped until someone notices.

This pattern has bitten downstream consumers of the template repeatedly:

- [`link-assistant/hive-mind` issue #1593](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1593) — original concurrent-merge race.
- [`link-assistant/hive-mind` issue #1730](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1730) — same shape, single PR adding a small block to many files near the threshold; case study at <https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/main/docs/case-studies/issue-1730/README.md>.

## Reproduction

1. In any repo using this template, prepare two PRs that each modify the same `.mjs` file.
2. Each PR alone leaves the file under 1500 lines (e.g. file is at 1499, PR-A adds 1 line, PR-B adds 1 line).
3. Merge PR-A. Merge PR-B. Both CI runs were green pre-merge.
4. The first CI run on `main` after PR-B fails: `ERROR: file has 1500 lines (limit: 1500)` (or above).
5. Release / build jobs are skipped because `check-file-line-limits` is a required gate.

## Suggested fix

Port the `WARN_THRESHOLD=1350` band from `link-assistant/hive-mind`'s version of the script. The 150-line gap (10% headroom) is enough to absorb typical concurrent-PR drift without being so tight that benign refactors are noisy.

Patch (functionally what hive-mind does today):

```bash
LIMIT=1500
WARN_THRESHOLD=1350
FAILURES=()
WARNINGS=()

# inside the find loop, after the >LIMIT check:
elif [ "\$line_count" -gt "\$WARN_THRESHOLD" ]; then
  WARNINGS+=(\"\$file\")
fi

# after the loop, before the failure summary:
if [ \"\${#WARNINGS[@]}\" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo \"\"
  echo \"The following files are approaching the \${LIMIT} line limit (>\${WARN_THRESHOLD} lines):\"
  printf '  %s\n' \"\${WARNINGS[@]}\"
  echo \"\"
  echo \"Consider extracting code to prevent concurrent PR merge limit violations.\"
fi
```

Hive-mind's version is here for reference: <https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/main/scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh>.

Optionally, project owners can promote warnings to errors in PRs that touch warn-band files, but the warn-only behavior is sufficient on its own — it gives reviewers a visible cue to pause and refactor before the file becomes a concurrent-merge hazard.

## Out-of-scope follow-up

While auditing the template against the same downstream incident, I noticed:

- The template's `eslint.config.js` already uses the shorthand `'max-lines': ['error', 1500]` (defaults `skipBlankLines: false, skipComments: false`), so it does **not** have the ESLint-vs-CI desync bug we just fixed in hive-mind. No change needed there.

Just adding the warn band would close the remaining gap.

comments.body:
Use all the best practices from CI/CD templates (check full file tree to compare for all GitHub workflow and CI/CD scripts file), if the same issue is found in template report issue also in templates:
- https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template

We should compare all files, so we don't have more CI/CD errors in the future and reuse all the best practices from these templates.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

title: check-file-line-limits.sh: add 1350 warn threshold to mitigate concurrent-PR merge race
